title: Add automatic chest-based overflow inventory management during gather/build
description:
## Problem
When gathering resources in survival mode, the bot's inventory fills up quickly. Currently it either stops gathering or the player must manually manage inventory. The agent has no concept of using chests as overflow storage.

## Agent Request
From session log: "automatic inventory management" and "direct integration for placing blocks from inventory".

## Scope
- When inventory is full (or nearly full) during a gather operation, the agent should:
  1. Navigate to the nearest known chest (or place one if none exists)
  2. Deposit overflow items
  3. Resume the gather goal
- Track known chest locations in the agent's knowledge base
- "Deposit all" / "Withdraw for build" chat commands
- LLM prompt updated so the agent can explain its inventory state

## Implementation sketch
1. Add `FindNearestChest` / `PlaceChest` action handlers in the Mineflayer adapter
2. Add `DepositOverflowTool` that checks inventory capacity and deposits to nearest chest
3. Store chest locations as world facts via `IWorldModel`
4. In `GatherGoalDecomposer` (or the dispatch loop), check inventory capacity before each gather cycle
5. Add chat commands: "deposit all [item]", "withdraw [item] [n]", "where is my chest?"

## Dependencies
TSK-0201 (chest interaction) provides the basic open/deposit/withdraw capability. This task adds automated overflow management on top.

## Complexity
Medium-High. Builds on TSK-0201 with additional automation logic.

## Priority
Low — survival-mode optimization.
